library(tidyverse)
## ── Attaching core tidyverse packages ──────────────────────── tidyverse 2.0.0 ──
## ✔ dplyr     1.2.0     ✔ readr     2.2.0
## ✔ forcats   1.0.1     ✔ stringr   1.6.0

## ✔ purrr     1.2.1     
## ── Conflicts ────────────────────────────────────────── tidyverse_conflicts() ──

## ✖ dplyr::lag()    masks stats::lag()

library(vars)

library(tseries)

##   method            from
##   as.zoo.data.frame zoo
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)
## 
## Attaching package: 'patchwork'
## 
## The following object is masked from 'package:MASS':
## 
##     area
library(scales)
## 
## Attaching package: 'scales'
## 
## The following object is masked from 'package:purrr':
## 
##     discard
## 
## The following object is masked from 'package:readr':
## 
##     col_factor
library(nortest)

select <- dplyr::select
INPUT_FILE <- "../../data/processed/merged_features_weekly.csv"
OUTPUT_DIR <- "outputs"
FREQ       <- 52
ALPHA      <- 0.05
MAX_LAG    <- 8

VAR_VARS <- c(
  "target_price",
  "api_energy_and_lubricants",
  "api_fertilisers_and_soil_improvers",
  "api_plant_protection_products",
  "api_fresh_fruit",
  "api_fresh_vegetables",
  "fuel_petrol_price",
  "fuel_diesel_price",
  "sppi_road_freight"
)

EXOG_VARS <- c("shock_2021q4_2023q1", "post_shock")
df <- read_csv(INPUT_FILE, show_col_types = FALSE) %>%
  mutate(date = as.Date(date)) %>%
  arrange(date)

target_commodities <- c(
  "Beetroot",
  "bulb onion yellow",
  "cabbage",
  "carrot",
  "cucumber",
  "curly kale",
  "lettuce",
  "tomato round"
)

df <- df %>%
  filter(commodity %in% target_commodities)

commodities <- unique(df$commodity)
cat("Filtered commodities:", paste(commodities, collapse = ", "), "\n")
## Filtered commodities: Beetroot
calc_rmse  <- function(actual, pred) sqrt(mean((actual - pred)^2, na.rm = TRUE))
calc_mae   <- function(actual, pred) mean(abs(actual - pred), na.rm = TRUE)
calc_mape  <- function(actual, pred) mean(abs((actual - pred) / actual) * 100, na.rm = TRUE)
calc_smape <- function(actual, pred) {
  mean(200 * abs(actual - pred) / (abs(actual) + abs(pred)), na.rm = TRUE)
}
calc_mase  <- function(actual, pred, train) {
  naive_mae <- mean(abs(diff(train)), na.rm = TRUE)
  mean(abs(actual - pred), na.rm = TRUE) / naive_mae
}

compile_metrics <- function(actual, pred, train, aic_val, bic_val, label) {
  tibble(
    model = label,
    RMSE  = round(calc_rmse(actual, pred),        4),
    MAE   = round(calc_mae(actual, pred),         4),
    MASE  = round(calc_mase(actual, pred, train), 4),
    sMAPE = round(calc_smape(actual, pred),       4),
    MAPE  = round(calc_mape(actual, pred),        4),
    AIC   = round(aic_val,                        2),
    BIC   = round(bic_val,                        2)
  )
}

make_stationary <- function(x, name, alpha = 0.05) {
  s <- x
  for (d in 0:2) {
    pval <- tryCatch(adf.test(s[!is.na(s)])$p.value, error = function(e) 1)
    if (pval < alpha) return(list(series = s, d = d))
    s <- c(NA, diff(s))
  }
  list(series = s, d = 2)
}

residual_diagnostics_var <- function(resid_vec, label) {
  resid_clean <- resid_vec[!is.na(resid_vec)]
  lb10 <- Box.test(resid_clean, lag = 10, type = "Ljung-Box")
  lb20 <- Box.test(resid_clean, lag = 20, type = "Ljung-Box")
  norm_test <- if (length(resid_clean) <= 5000) {
    list(result = shapiro.test(resid_clean), name = "Shapiro-Wilk")
  } else {
    list(result = nortest::ad.test(resid_clean), name = "Anderson-Darling")
  }
  tibble(
    model             = label,
    resid_mean        = round(mean(resid_clean),           6),
    resid_sd          = round(sd(resid_clean),             4),
    ljung_box_lag10_p = round(lb10$p.value,                4),
    ljung_box_lag20_p = round(lb20$p.value,                4),
    normality_test    = norm_test$name,
    normality_p       = round(norm_test$result$p.value,    4),
    autocorr_ok       = lb10$p.value > ALPHA,
    normality_ok      = norm_test$result$p.value > ALPHA
  )
}

plot_residuals <- function(resid_vec, fitted_vec, label) {
  resid_clean <- resid_vec[!is.na(resid_vec)]
  time_idx    <- seq_along(resid_vec)
  ci_bound    <- qnorm(0.975) / sqrt(length(resid_clean))

  p1 <- ggplot(data.frame(t = time_idx, r = resid_vec), aes(t, r)) +
    geom_line(color = "#2c3e50", linewidth = 0.45) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "red", linetype = "dashed") +
    labs(title = "Residuals over time", x = "Week index", y = "Residual") +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 11)

  acf_data <- acf(resid_clean, plot = FALSE, lag.max = 52)
  acf_df   <- data.frame(lag = acf_data$lag[-1], acf = acf_data$acf[-1])
  p2 <- ggplot(acf_df, aes(lag, acf)) +
    geom_segment(aes(xend = lag, yend = 0), color = "#2980b9") +
    geom_hline(yintercept = c(-ci_bound, ci_bound), linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "black") +
    labs(title = "ACF of residuals", x = "Lag (weeks)", y = "ACF") +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 11)

  pacf_data <- pacf(resid_clean, plot = FALSE, lag.max = 52)
  pacf_df   <- data.frame(lag = pacf_data$lag, pacf = pacf_data$acf)
  p3 <- ggplot(pacf_df, aes(lag, pacf)) +
    geom_segment(aes(xend = lag, yend = 0), color = "#8e44ad") +
    geom_hline(yintercept = c(-ci_bound, ci_bound), linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "black") +
    labs(title = "PACF of residuals", x = "Lag (weeks)", y = "PACF") +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 11)

  p4 <- ggplot(data.frame(r = resid_clean), aes(r)) +
    geom_histogram(aes(y = after_stat(density)), bins = 40,
                   fill = "#3498db", color = "white", alpha = 0.7) +
    stat_function(fun = dnorm,
                  args = list(mean = mean(resid_clean), sd = sd(resid_clean)),
                  color = "red", linewidth = 1) +
    labs(title = "Residual distribution", x = "Residual", y = "Density") +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 11)

  qq    <- qqnorm(resid_clean, plot.it = FALSE)
  qq_df <- data.frame(theoretical = qq$x, sample = qq$y)
  p5 <- ggplot(qq_df, aes(theoretical, sample)) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.4, color = "#2c3e50", size = 0.8) +
    geom_abline(color = "red", linewidth = 1) +
    labs(title = "Q-Q plot", x = "Theoretical quantiles", y = "Sample quantiles") +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 11)

  p6 <- ggplot(data.frame(f = fitted_vec, r = resid_vec), aes(f, r)) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.35, size = 0.9, color = "#16a085") +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "red", linetype = "dashed") +
    geom_smooth(method = "loess", formula = y ~ x, se = FALSE,
                color = "#e67e22", linewidth = 0.8) +
    labs(title = "Residuals vs Fitted", x = "Fitted values", y = "Residual") +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 11)

  combined <- (p1 | p4) / (p2 | p3) / (p5 | p6) +
    plot_annotation(
      title    = paste0("Residual Diagnostics (target_price equation) — ", label),
      subtitle = "Row 1: Time series & distribution | Row 2: ACF & PACF | Row 3: Q-Q & Residuals vs Fitted",
      theme    = theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13))
    )
  print(combined)
}

plot_forecast <- function(train_dates, train_vals,
                          test_dates,  test_vals, pred_vals, label) {
  cutoff   <- train_dates[length(train_dates)] - 365 * 2
  mask     <- train_dates >= cutoff
  train_df <- data.frame(date = train_dates[mask], value = train_vals[mask])
  test_df  <- data.frame(date = test_dates,  value = test_vals)
  pred_df  <- data.frame(date = test_dates,  value = pred_vals)

  p <- ggplot() +
    geom_line(data = train_df, aes(x = date, y = value),
              color = "#bdc3c7", linewidth = 0.9) +
    geom_line(data = test_df,  aes(x = date, y = value, color = "Actual"),
              linewidth = 1.2) +
    geom_line(data = pred_df,  aes(x = date, y = value, color = "VAR forecast"),
              linewidth = 1.2, linetype = "dashed") +
    scale_color_manual(
      values = c("Actual" = "#2c3e50", "VAR forecast" = "#e74c3c"), name = NULL
    ) +
    labs(
      title    = paste0("VAR Forecast — ", label),
      subtitle = "Dashed = forecast | Grey = last 2 years of training",
      x = NULL, y = "Price"
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"), legend.position = "bottom")
  print(p)
}

plot_irf <- function(model_obj, label, n_ahead = 26) {
  irf_plots    <- list()
  impulse_vars <- colnames(model_obj$y)
  impulse_vars <- impulse_vars[impulse_vars != "target_price"]

  for (imp in impulse_vars) {
    irf_res <- tryCatch(
      irf(model_obj, impulse = imp, response = "target_price",
          n.ahead = n_ahead, boot = TRUE, ci = 0.95, runs = 100),
      error = function(e) NULL
    )
    if (is.null(irf_res)) next

    irf_df <- data.frame(
      horizon = 0:n_ahead,
      irf     = as.numeric(irf_res$irf[[1]]),
      lo      = as.numeric(irf_res$Lower[[1]]),
      hi      = as.numeric(irf_res$Upper[[1]])
    )

    irf_plots[[imp]] <- ggplot(irf_df, aes(x = horizon)) +
      geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lo, ymax = hi), fill = "#3498db", alpha = 0.2) +
      geom_line(aes(y = irf), color = "#2c3e50", linewidth = 0.9) +
      geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
      labs(title = imp, x = "Weeks ahead", y = "Response") +
      theme_minimal(base_size = 10) +
      theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 9, face = "bold"))
  }

  if (length(irf_plots) == 0) return(invisible(NULL))

  n_cols   <- 3
  combined <- wrap_plots(irf_plots, ncol = n_cols) +
    plot_annotation(
      title    = paste0("Impulse Response Functions → target_price\n", label),
      subtitle = "Shaded = 95% bootstrap CI (100 runs)",
      theme    = theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13))
    )
  print(combined)
}

run_granger <- function(model_obj, label) {
  vars_in_model <- colnames(model_obj$y)
  other_vars    <- vars_in_model[vars_in_model != "target_price"]
  map_dfr(other_vars, function(v) {
    res <- tryCatch(causality(model_obj, cause = v), error = function(e) NULL)
    if (is.null(res)) return(NULL)
    tibble(
      model       = label,
      cause       = v,
      f_stat      = round(as.numeric(res$Granger$statistic), 4),
      p_value     = round(as.numeric(res$Granger$p.value),   4),
      significant = as.numeric(res$Granger$p.value) < ALPHA
    )
  })
}
all_metrics <- list()
all_diag    <- list()
all_granger <- list()
for (comm in commodities) {
  cat("\n", strrep("=", 60), "\n")
  cat("  Commodity:", comm, "\n")
  cat(strrep("=", 60), "\n")

  safe_name <- str_replace_all(comm, "[ /]", "_")

  sub <- df %>% filter(commodity == comm) %>% arrange(date)

  if (!"split" %in% names(sub)) stop("'split' column not found in data.")

  train_df <- sub %>% filter(split == "train")
  test_df  <- sub %>% filter(split == "test")

  if (nrow(train_df) < 30 || nrow(test_df) == 0) {
    cat("  !! Insufficient rows — skipping.\n"); next
  }

  n_test <- nrow(test_df)
  cat(sprintf("  Train: %s -> %s (%d weeks)\n",
              min(train_df$date), max(train_df$date), nrow(train_df)))
  cat(sprintf("  Test:  %s -> %s (%d weeks)\n",
              min(test_df$date),  max(test_df$date),  n_test))

  avail_vars <- VAR_VARS[VAR_VARS %in% names(sub) &
                           sapply(VAR_VARS, function(v) sum(!is.na(train_df[[v]])) > MAX_LAG + 5)]
  if (!"target_price" %in% avail_vars) {
    cat("  !! target_price has too many NAs — skipping.\n"); next
  }
  avail_vars <- c("target_price", avail_vars[avail_vars != "target_price"])
  cat("  VAR variables:", paste(avail_vars, collapse = ", "), "\n")

  avail_exog <- EXOG_VARS[EXOG_VARS %in% names(sub)]
  cat("  Exogenous:    ", paste(avail_exog, collapse = ", "), "\n")

  train_mat <- train_df %>%
    select(all_of(avail_vars)) %>%
    mutate(across(everything(), ~ ifelse(is.na(.), mean(., na.rm = TRUE), .)))

  d_orders   <- setNames(integer(length(avail_vars)), avail_vars)
  train_stat <- train_mat

  for (v in avail_vars) {
    res             <- make_stationary(train_mat[[v]], v, ALPHA)
    train_stat[[v]] <- res$series
    d_orders[v]     <- res$d
  }

  max_d      <- max(d_orders)
  train_stat <- train_stat %>% drop_na()
  cat(sprintf("  Max differencing order: %d  |  Usable train rows: %d\n",
              max_d, nrow(train_stat)))

  exog_train <- if (length(avail_exog) > 0) {
    train_df %>%
      select(all_of(avail_exog)) %>%
      tail(nrow(train_stat)) %>%
      as.matrix()
  } else NULL

  cat("  Selecting lag order (VARselect, max =", MAX_LAG, ")...\n")
  lag_sel <- tryCatch(
    VARselect(train_stat, lag.max = MAX_LAG, type = "const", exogen = exog_train),
    error = function(e) { cat("  !! VARselect failed:", e$message, "\n"); NULL }
  )
  if (is.null(lag_sel)) next

  best_lag_bic <- lag_sel$selection["SC(n)"]
  chosen_lag   <- best_lag_bic
  cat(sprintf("  Lag selected — AIC: %d  |  BIC: %d\n",
              lag_sel$selection["AIC(n)"], best_lag_bic))
  cat(sprintf("  Using BIC lag: %d\n", chosen_lag))

  cat("  Fitting VAR(", chosen_lag, ")...\n")
  model_var <- tryCatch(
    VAR(train_stat, p = chosen_lag, type = "const", exogen = exog_train),
    error = function(e) { cat("  !! VAR fit failed:", e$message, "\n"); NULL }
  )
  if (is.null(model_var)) next

  cat(sprintf("  AIC: %.2f  |  BIC: %.2f\n", AIC(model_var), BIC(model_var)))

  cat("  Walk-forward forecasting", n_test, "steps...\n")
  pred_vals <- numeric(n_test)
  all_data  <- bind_rows(train_df, test_df) %>%
    select(all_of(c(avail_vars, avail_exog))) %>%
    mutate(across(everything(), ~ ifelse(is.na(.), mean(., na.rm = TRUE), .)))

  for (i in seq_len(n_test)) {
    wf_train <- all_data[1 : (nrow(train_df) + i - 1), avail_vars, drop = FALSE]

    wf_stat <- wf_train
    for (v in avail_vars) {
      if (d_orders[v] > 0) {
        for (dd in seq_len(d_orders[v])) wf_stat[[v]] <- c(NA, diff(wf_stat[[v]]))
      }
    }
    wf_stat <- drop_na(wf_stat)

    wf_exog <- if (length(avail_exog) > 0)
      as.matrix(all_data[seq_len(nrow(train_df) + i - 1), avail_exog] %>% tail(nrow(wf_stat)))
    else NULL

    wf_exog_fc <- if (length(avail_exog) > 0)
      as.matrix(all_data[nrow(train_df) + i, avail_exog])
    else NULL

    m <- tryCatch(
      VAR(wf_stat, p = chosen_lag, type = "const", exogen = wf_exog),
      error = function(e) NULL
    )
    if (is.null(m)) { pred_vals[i] <- NA; next }

    fc <- tryCatch(predict(m, n.ahead = 1, dumvar = wf_exog_fc), error = function(e) NULL)
    if (is.null(fc)) { pred_vals[i] <- NA; next }

    pred_diff <- fc$fcst$target_price[1, "fcst"]

    if (d_orders["target_price"] >= 1) {
      pred_vals[i] <- wf_train$target_price[nrow(wf_train)] + pred_diff
    } else {
      pred_vals[i] <- pred_diff
    }

    if (d_orders["target_price"] == 2) {
      last_fd      <- diff(wf_train$target_price)
      last_fd      <- last_fd[length(last_fd)]
      pred_vals[i] <- wf_train$target_price[nrow(wf_train)] + last_fd + pred_diff
    }

    if (i %% 10 == 0) cat(sprintf("    Step %d/%d\n", i, n_test))
  }

  actual <- test_df$target_price
  valid  <- !is.na(pred_vals) & !is.na(actual)

  metrics <- compile_metrics(
    actual  = actual[valid], pred    = pred_vals[valid],
    train   = train_df$target_price,
    aic_val = AIC(model_var), bic_val = BIC(model_var),
    label   = paste0(comm, " — VAR(", chosen_lag, ")")
  )
  cat("\n  METRICS:\n")
  for (col in c("RMSE","MAE","MASE","sMAPE","MAPE","AIC","BIC"))
    cat(sprintf("    %-8s: %s\n", col, metrics[[col]]))

  all_metrics[[comm]] <- metrics

  resid_target  <- as.numeric(residuals(model_var)[, "target_price"])
  fitted_target <- as.numeric(fitted(model_var)[, "target_price"])

  all_diag[[comm]] <- residual_diagnostics_var(
    resid_target, paste0(comm, " — VAR(", chosen_lag, ")")
  )

  gc_res <- run_granger(model_var, paste0(comm, " — VAR(", chosen_lag, ")"))
  if (!is.null(gc_res) && nrow(gc_res) > 0) {
    all_granger[[comm]] <- gc_res
    cat("\n  Granger causality (-> target_price):\n")
    print(gc_res %>% select(cause, f_stat, p_value, significant), n = Inf)
  }

  plot_residuals(resid_target, fitted_target, paste0(comm, " — VAR(", chosen_lag, ")"))

  plot_forecast(
    train_dates = train_df$date,  train_vals = train_df$target_price,
    test_dates  = test_df$date,   test_vals  = actual,
    pred_vals   = pred_vals,      label      = paste0(comm, " — VAR(", chosen_lag, ")")
  )

  plot_irf(model_obj = model_var, label = paste0(comm, " — VAR(", chosen_lag, ")"))
}
## 
##  ============================================================ 
##   Commodity: Beetroot 
## ============================================================ 
##   Train: 2016-01-22 -> 2024-12-20 (446 weeks)
##   Test:  2025-01-20 -> 2026-02-16 (28 weeks)
##   VAR variables: target_price, api_energy_and_lubricants, api_fertilisers_and_soil_improvers, api_plant_protection_products, api_fresh_fruit, api_fresh_vegetables, fuel_petrol_price, fuel_diesel_price, sppi_road_freight 
##   Exogenous:     shock_2021q4_2023q1, post_shock
## Warning in adf.test(s[!is.na(s)]): p-value smaller than printed p-value
## Warning in adf.test(s[!is.na(s)]): p-value smaller than printed p-value
## Warning in adf.test(s[!is.na(s)]): p-value smaller than printed p-value
## Warning in adf.test(s[!is.na(s)]): p-value smaller than printed p-value
## Warning in adf.test(s[!is.na(s)]): p-value smaller than printed p-value
## Warning in adf.test(s[!is.na(s)]): p-value smaller than printed p-value
## Warning in adf.test(s[!is.na(s)]): p-value smaller than printed p-value
## Warning in adf.test(s[!is.na(s)]): p-value smaller than printed p-value
## Warning in adf.test(s[!is.na(s)]): p-value smaller than printed p-value
##   Max differencing order: 1  |  Usable train rows: 445
##   Selecting lag order (VARselect, max = 8 )...
##   Lag selected — AIC: 1  |  BIC: 1
##   Using BIC lag: 1
##   Fitting VAR( 1 )...
##   AIC: 13598.22  |  BIC: 14040.57
##   Walk-forward forecasting 28 steps...
##     Step 10/28
##     Step 20/28
## 
##   METRICS:
##     RMSE    : 0.0448
##     MAE     : 0.0311
##     MASE    : 1.6457
##     sMAPE   : 4.1827
##     MAPE    : 4.1561
##     AIC     : 13598.22
##     BIC     : 14040.57
## 
##   Granger causality (-> target_price):
## # A tibble: 8 × 4
##   cause                              f_stat p_value significant
##   <chr>                               <dbl>   <dbl> <lgl>      
## 1 api_energy_and_lubricants          0.0228   1     FALSE      
## 2 api_fertilisers_and_soil_improvers 0.019    1     FALSE      
## 3 api_plant_protection_products      0.185    0.993 FALSE      
## 4 api_fresh_fruit                    0.174    0.994 FALSE      
## 5 api_fresh_vegetables               0.253    0.980 FALSE      
## 6 fuel_petrol_price                  0.203    0.990 FALSE      
## 7 fuel_diesel_price                  0.160    0.996 FALSE      
## 8 sppi_road_freight                  0.181    0.994 FALSE

metrics_df <- bind_rows(all_metrics)
diag_df    <- bind_rows(all_diag)
granger_df <- bind_rows(all_granger)

cat("\n\n", strrep("=", 70), "\n")
## 
## 
##  ======================================================================
cat("  VAR MODEL PERFORMANCE METRICS\n")
##   VAR MODEL PERFORMANCE METRICS
cat(strrep("=", 70), "\n")
## ======================================================================
print(metrics_df, n = Inf)
## # A tibble: 1 × 8
##   model               RMSE    MAE  MASE sMAPE  MAPE    AIC    BIC
##   <chr>              <dbl>  <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>  <dbl>  <dbl>
## 1 Beetroot — VAR(1) 0.0448 0.0311  1.65  4.18  4.16 13598. 14041.
cat("\n\n", strrep("=", 70), "\n")
## 
## 
##  ======================================================================
cat("  RESIDUAL DIAGNOSTICS (target_price equation)\n")
##   RESIDUAL DIAGNOSTICS (target_price equation)
cat(strrep("=", 70), "\n")
## ======================================================================
print(diag_df, n = Inf)
## # A tibble: 1 × 9
##   model   resid_mean resid_sd ljung_box_lag10_p ljung_box_lag20_p normality_test
##   <chr>        <dbl>    <dbl>             <dbl>             <dbl> <chr>         
## 1 Beetro…          0   0.0298            0.0017            0.0038 Shapiro-Wilk  
## # ℹ 3 more variables: normality_p <dbl>, autocorr_ok <lgl>, normality_ok <lgl>
if (nrow(granger_df) > 0) {
  cat("\n\n", strrep("=", 70), "\n")
  cat("  GRANGER CAUSALITY: variables -> target_price\n")
  cat(strrep("=", 70), "\n")
  print(granger_df %>% arrange(model, p_value), n = Inf)
}
## 
## 
##  ====================================================================== 
##   GRANGER CAUSALITY: variables -> target_price
## ====================================================================== 
## # A tibble: 8 × 5
##   model             cause                             f_stat p_value significant
##   <chr>             <chr>                              <dbl>   <dbl> <lgl>      
## 1 Beetroot — VAR(1) api_fresh_vegetables              0.253    0.980 FALSE      
## 2 Beetroot — VAR(1) fuel_petrol_price                 0.203    0.990 FALSE      
## 3 Beetroot — VAR(1) api_plant_protection_products     0.185    0.993 FALSE      
## 4 Beetroot — VAR(1) sppi_road_freight                 0.181    0.994 FALSE      
## 5 Beetroot — VAR(1) api_fresh_fruit                   0.174    0.994 FALSE      
## 6 Beetroot — VAR(1) fuel_diesel_price                 0.160    0.996 FALSE      
## 7 Beetroot — VAR(1) api_energy_and_lubricants         0.0228   1     FALSE      
## 8 Beetroot — VAR(1) api_fertilisers_and_soil_improve… 0.019    1     FALSE
write_csv(metrics_df, file.path(OUTPUT_DIR, "var_metrics_filtered.csv"))
write_csv(diag_df,    file.path(OUTPUT_DIR, "var_residual_diagnostics_filtered.csv"))
if (nrow(granger_df) > 0)
  write_csv(granger_df, file.path(OUTPUT_DIR, "var_granger_causality_filtered.csv"))

cat("\n✅ Done.\n")
## 
## ✅ Done.
